docs.text: Welcome to gitlab-releases documentation This site contains the project documentation for the gitlab-releases project, a Django reusable app to display and manage GitLab release information in your Django projects. Table of contents How-To Guides Introduction Main features: Display a list of releases from your GitLab repository Show detailed changelog for each release Integrate release data into your Django templates Custom template tags for advanced rendering Simple configuration and setup Get started Install gitlab-releases in your virtual environment: pip install gitlab-releases Add it to your INSTALLED_APPS in settings.py : INSTALLED_APPS = [ # ...existing apps... 'gitlab_releases', ] Include the app's URLs in your urls.py : from django.urls import path, include urlpatterns = [ # ...existing urls... path('releases/', include('gitlab_releases.urls')), ] Visit /releases/ in your Django project to see the list of releases. Useful links Documentation Changelog PyPI Page Repository Bug Tracker
docs.title: gitlab-releases

docs.title: Welcome to gitlab-releases documentation

docs.text: Main features: Display a list of releases from your GitLab repository Show detailed changelog for each release Integrate release data into your Django templates Custom template tags for advanced rendering Simple configuration and setup
docs.title: Introduction

docs.text: Install gitlab-releases in your virtual environment: pip install gitlab-releases Add it to your INSTALLED_APPS in settings.py : INSTALLED_APPS = [ # ...existing apps... 'gitlab_releases', ] Include the app's URLs in your urls.py : from django.urls import path, include urlpatterns = [ # ...existing urls... path('releases/', include('gitlab_releases.urls')), ] Visit /releases/ in your Django project to see the list of releases.

docs.text:
def my_upload_generator(instance, filename): How-to guides This section provides practical guides for using gitlab-releases in your Django project. Displaying GitLab Releases in Your Django App After installing and configuring gitlab-releases, you can display your GitLab releases in your Django project with minimal setup. 1. Add to Installed Apps In your settings.py : INSTALLED_APPS = [ # ...existing apps... 'gitlab_releases', ] 2. Include URLs In your urls.py : from django.urls import path, include urlpatterns = [ # ...existing urls... path('releases/', include('gitlab_releases.urls')), ] 3. Access the Release List Visit /releases/ in your browser to see the list of releases fetched from your GitLab repository. Customizing Templates You can use the provided template tags to render release data in your own templates. For example: {% load gitlab_releases_filters %} {% for release in releases %}

{{ release.title }}

{{ release.description|markdown }}
{% endfor %} Overriding Templates To customize the look and feel, override the default templates by copying them from gitlab_releases/templates/gitlab_releases/ into your own app's templates directory and editing as needed. Filtering or Sorting Releases You can filter or sort the releases in your views or templates using standard Django template logic or by extending the app's views. Advanced: Using the API in Your Own Views If you want to fetch and display releases programmatically, you can use the internal client: from gitlab_releases.client import GitlabReleasesClient client = GitlabReleasesClient() releases = client.get_releases(project_id="your-gitlab-project-id") # Use 'releases' in your custom view or context For more advanced usage and integration, see the full documentation and API reference.
docs.title: How-To Guides
